prompt:
Our artifact prefetcher (this package) has been racking up incident notes and I've run out of patience, so here is everything ops has on it.

One: retry pacing is configured to start at 100ms per file and double per retry of that same file. In reality the waits climb across the whole run. Staging log excerpt: pkg-a retry #1 in 100ms, pkg-a retry #2 in 200ms, then pkg-b retry #1 in 400ms, pkg-b retry #2 in 800ms — and by the tail of a big run, files sit several seconds before their FIRST retry. Every file was supposed to pace itself independently.

Two: twice now the daemon died with a runtime fatal error at the exact moment the dashboard polled the status snapshot while a run was in flight. We cannot reproduce it on demand — it's rare — but when it hits, it takes the whole process down, not just the one request.

Three: any run bigger than one batch stalls. The first BatchSize files download fine, then nothing: no errors, no retries, CPU idle, and a stack dump shows everything parked and waiting. Runs that fit inside a single batch complete normally, which is exactly why the small repos never see it and the monorepo mirror hits it every night.

go test -race -timeout 120s ./... fails against this package and pins the behavior we want. Please figure out what's actually wrong and fix the package — dlpool_test.go is off limits.
